title: Humea elata
intro: Humea elata is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India, belonging to the family Celastraceae. Traditionally, Humea elata has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Humea elata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Humea elata is often used to prepare herbal decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin problems.
taxonomy: Humea elata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the staff vine family. This family is a part of the larger order Celastrales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Celastraceae family is further divided into several genera, with Humea being one of the lesser-known ones. Humea elata is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and is a relatively rare species. The exact classification of Humea elata is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids.
properties: Unfortunately, I could not find any information about the therapeutic properties of Humea elata.
